name: Radicchio and Goat Cheese Gratin with Balsami
recipeIngredient: 8 oz soft goat cheese (preferably Coach Farm)
1 tbsp fresh rosemary leaves, finely chopped
2 tbsp balsamic vinegar plus 4 tablespoons additional for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
2. In a large 12 to 14-inch sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
3. Place the radicchio quarters in the oil (4 at a time) cut side down and sauté until lightly brown on both cut sides. Set aside and repeat with the remaining 4 quarters. Let cool.
4. While the pan is still hot, saute the sage leaves until crisp and brown, set aside.
5. In a mixing bowl, stir together the goat cheese, rosemary, egg, Parmesan, and 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar until well blended.
6. Pack about 3 tablespoons of the cheese mixture on top of each radicchio quarter, smoothing over to form a slightly rounded top.
7. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until the goat cheese has started to brown slightly. Remove from oven and sprinkle with remaining balsamic vinegar and garnish with 2 sage leaves each. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.
8. Yield: 4 servings as an antipasto
